description: Introduce LearningWriter facade as the common chokepoint for creating learnings + scheduling embedding refresh. Migrate all four production creation paths (learn, import-learnings, curate_dedup/merge_cluster, extract_learnings_from_output) to use it. Goal: a future dev cannot add a fifth creation path and silently forget the embedding step.

criteria: cargo check passes with zero warnings
cargo test passes — all ~2854 existing tests still green plus new tests
No new unwrap() in production code paths (tests OK); prefer .expect("invariant reason")
record_learning stays public — do not rename, remove, or change its signature

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Approach before code. Anticipate edge cases. Consider 2-3 approaches, pick the best
2. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable code, wired in according to the plan
3. CORRECTNESS — Self-critique after code. Compiles, type-checks, all tests pass
4. CODE QUALITY — Quality dimensions explicit. Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — read qualityDimensions on every task; know what 'good' looks like before coding
Approach before code — consider 2-3 approaches with tradeoffs, pick the best, then implement
Each task delivers working code + tests together
Self-critique after code — verify all acceptance criteria, especially negative ones
Ollama HTTP calls MUST NOT happen inside SQLite transactions — verify this invariant in every path
Handle Option/Result explicitly; prefer .expect("invariant") over .unwrap() in production paths

prohibitedOutcomes: Any production creation path that bypasses LearningWriter and calls record_learning directly
Ollama HTTP calls issued inside an active rusqlite Transaction (would hold DB locks during network I/O)
LearningWriter that panics from Drop (stack-unwind UB) — use eprintln! only
Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
MergeClusterResult missing the new merged_title/merged_content fields that curate_dedup needs
Silent failures: embedding errors must log to stderr with learning_id context
Breaking changes to record_learning signature or behavior — 25+ test files depend on it

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: src/learnings/crud/writer.rs exists with `pub struct LearningWriter<'a>` holding `db_dir: Option<&'a Path>` and `pending: Vec<PendingEmbed>`
Positive: `pub fn new(db_dir: Option<&'a Path>) -> Self` constructor

Positive: `pub fn record(&mut self, conn: &Connection, params: RecordLearningParams) -> TaskMgrResult<RecordLearningResult>` — clones title/content, calls record_learning, pushes to pending only if db_dir.is_some()

Positive: `pub fn flush(mut self, conn: &Connection) -> usize` — consumes self; drains pending; calls try_embed_learnings_batch; returns stored count. No-op (returns 0) if db_dir is None or pending is empty.
Positive: Drop impl prints eprintln!("Warning: LearningWriter dropped with N un-flushed pending embedding(s). Call .flush() before dropping.") when pending is non-empty on drop
Positive: pub mod + pub use wired through src/learnings/crud/mod.rs AND src/learnings/mod.rs so callers can write `use crate::learnings::LearningWriter`

Positive: Doc comment on record() says: `Accepts &Connection or &Transaction (Transaction derefs to Connection). When inside a conn.transaction() block, pass &tx — not &conn — because conn is mutably borrowed.`

Positive: Doc comment on flush() says: `Consumes self to prevent reuse. Call AFTER any active transaction has committed. Ollama errors are swallowed; the returned count is advisory — callers can ignore it.`
Unit test: `test_record_without_db_dir_skips_pending` — new(None), record a learning, verify pending is empty (via flush returning 0)
Unit test: `test_record_with_db_dir_queues_pending` — new(Some(&tempdir)), record, verify flush would attempt embedding (use a tempdir where Ollama is unavailable — flush returns 0 gracefully)
Unit test: `test_flush_empty_returns_zero` — new(Some(dir)), no record calls, flush returns 0
Unit test: `test_record_inside_transaction` — open tx via conn.unchecked_transaction(), pass &tx to record(), commit tx, then flush

Unit test: `test_drop_warning_fires` — use std::panic::catch_unwind or scope + drop; verify the warning is emitted (can use gag crate or just `eprintln` + manual inspection — simplest: verify drop doesn't panic and pending.is_empty() after drain)
Negative: Drop does NOT panic — verified by a test that drops a writer with pending items inside a `#[test]` (a panicking Drop would abort the test)
Negative: No #[must_use] attribute on LearningWriter — it doesn't enforce what we want (it applies to return values, not dropped locals)
Known-bad: if record() is written to call record_learning AFTER pushing to pending, an error in record_learning would leak a phantom pending entry. Verify record_learning runs BEFORE the push.
CONTRACT: `try_embed_learnings_batch` in src/learnings/embeddings/mod.rs expects `&[(i64, String, String)]` — verify the flush() conversion matches this exact shape (grep the function signature)
Failure mode: if try_embed_learnings_batch returns an error internally, it already eprintln!s; flush() must NOT propagate and must still return a count (0 if all failed)
userStories.description:
Create the LearningWriter facade in a new file src/learnings/crud/writer.rs. This is the single chokepoint all production paths will use for creating learnings. It defers Ollama HTTP calls until flush() so they never run inside a SQLite transaction.

DO: Create src/learnings/crud/writer.rs with the LearningWriter struct, PendingEmbed private struct, record() method, flush(self) consuming method, and Drop impl that warns to stderr (NEVER panics).
DO: Export from src/learnings/crud/mod.rs via `pub mod writer; pub use writer::LearningWriter;`
DO: Re-export from src/learnings/mod.rs at the top level alongside the existing crud re-exports.
DO: Unit tests that cover: (a) record with db_dir=None does NOT populate pending (graceful no-op), (b) record with db_dir=Some populates pending, (c) flush with empty pending returns 0, (d) Drop with non-empty pending prints to stderr (use a scope + drop(writer) pattern), (e) record accepts both &Connection and &Transaction (call from inside a conn.unchecked_transaction()).
DO NOT: Add a panic! in Drop — panicking during stack unwinding is UB. Use eprintln! only.
DO NOT: Issue Ollama HTTP calls in record(); the whole point is to defer them to flush().
DO NOT: Make record_learning private or rename it — 25+ test files use it.
DO NOT: Try to abstract over Connection/Transaction with a trait; rusqlite Transaction derefs to Connection — pass &tx directly (learning 111).

Edge cases:
- db_dir=None: record() still calls record_learning() and returns the result, but does NOT push to pending. flush() returns 0.
- Empty pending on flush: return 0, no Ollama call attempted.
- Drop with un-flushed pending: print eprintln! warning with the count; do NOT panic.
- Transaction passthrough: caller passes &tx (Transaction derefs to Connection). Record works normally; the tx wrapping is the caller's concern.

Known-bad to guard against:
- A future dev adds `.flush()` that takes `&mut self` instead of `self` — this allows flush-then-record-then-forget, defeating the point. Use consuming `self`.
- A Drop impl that calls panic! instead of eprintln! — compiles fine but aborts the process on any error path that drops an un-flushed writer during stack unwinding.
edgeCases: db_dir=None: record succeeds, pending stays empty, flush returns 0
Empty pending on flush: return 0 without touching Ollama
Drop with non-empty pending: eprintln warning, never panic
record inside conn.unchecked_transaction(): &tx works via Deref
Ollama unreachable at flush time: try_embed_learnings_batch logs warning and returns 0

failureModes.expectedBehavior: return Err immediately; pending stays unchanged; no phantom entry

failureModes.cause: Ollama server down at flush() time
failureModes.expectedBehavior: try_embed_learnings_batch returns 0 (it already handles this); flush() returns 0; no error propagated

failureModes.cause: Writer dropped without flush() (e.g., early return on error)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Drop prints eprintln warning with count; never panics

correctness: record_learning is called BEFORE the push to pending — on DB error, pending stays clean
flush consumes self so writer cannot be reused after flushing
Drop never panics, even during stack unwinding
record accepts &Transaction via Deref — no extra trait bounds
performance: record() does 2 string clones (title, content) per call — acceptable, avoids lifetime complexity
flush() batches all pending embeddings into a single try_embed_learnings_batch call
style: Rustdoc on every pub item explaining the tx/conn rule
Match existing module style: pub struct, impl block, tests module

userStories.title: LearningWriter facade: struct, record(), flush(), Drop + unit tests

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: learn() signature is `fn learn(conn: &Connection, db_dir: Option<&Path>, params: LearnParams) -> TaskMgrResult<LearnResult>`
Positive: learn() imports `use crate::learnings::LearningWriter` (or `crud::LearningWriter`)
Positive: learn() body uses `let mut writer = LearningWriter::new(db_dir); let result = writer.record(conn, record_params)?; writer.flush(conn); Ok(LearnResult::from(result))`
Positive: src/main.rs passes `Some(&cli.dir)` at the learn() call in Commands::Learn arm
Positive: All 17 unit tests in src/commands/learn.rs updated to pass `None` as the 2nd argument — `learn(&conn, None, params)`
Positive: tests/e2e_loop.rs learn call updated with `None`
Positive: tests/learnings.rs learn call updated with `None`
Positive: `cargo test commands::learn` passes clean
Positive: `cargo test --test learnings` passes clean
Positive: `cargo test --test e2e_loop` passes clean
Negative: No direct call to `record_learning(conn, ...)` in learn.rs body — only through writer.record
Negative: No direct call to `try_embed_learning` anywhere in learn.rs
Known-bad: if flush() is accidentally placed AFTER the `Ok(LearnResult::from(result))` return, the writer drops without flushing — verify flush runs BEFORE the return
Known-bad: if any test caller is missed, cargo test fails with `this function takes 3 arguments but 2 arguments were supplied` — grep for `learn(&conn` across the whole repo to catch all callers
Failure mode: if writer.record returns Err, the `?` propagates it. Writer drops with empty pending (no embedding was queued yet). No stderr warning.
userStories.description:
Add a `db_dir: Option<&Path>` parameter to `learn()` and replace the current `record_learning` call with a `LearningWriter`. This is the simplest migration path — no transaction involved — and serves as a reference for the other migrations.

DO: Change `learn()` signature from `fn learn(conn: &Connection, params: LearnParams) -> TaskMgrResult<LearnResult>` to `fn learn(conn: &Connection, db_dir: Option<&Path>, params: LearnParams) -> TaskMgrResult<LearnResult>`.
DO: Inside `learn()`, construct `LearningWriter::new(db_dir)`, call `writer.record(conn, record_params)?`, then `writer.flush(conn)` before returning.
DO: Update ALL callers to pass the new param:
- `src/main.rs` (Commands::Learn arm, ~line 345): pass `Some(&cli.dir)`
- 17 unit tests inside `src/commands/learn.rs` (calls like `learn(&conn, params)`): pass `None`
- 2 integration tests: `tests/e2e_loop.rs` (~line 186) and `tests/learnings.rs` (~line 1028): pass `None`
DO: Remove the existing auto-embed helper imports from main.rs's Learn arm (if any become dead).
DO NOT: Change the LearnResult type or the auto-populate logic for applies_to_files/applies_to_task_types.
DO NOT: Propagate errors from writer.flush — flush returns usize (best-effort), never Err.

Edge cases:
- db_dir=None (tests): writer.record() still runs record_learning; flush() returns 0. Net effect matches old behavior.
- Ollama down, db_dir=Some: flush() logs warning, returns 0. learn() still returns the successful LearnResult.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Calling writer.flush(conn) BEFORE writer.record(conn, params) — no-op but obviously wrong.
- Forgetting to call writer.flush() — Drop warns but embeddings are lost for this run. Verify flush() is called on every code path that returns Ok.
edgeCases: db_dir=None (tests): record runs, flush returns 0, LearnResult returned normally
db_dir=Some, Ollama down: flush logs warning, returns 0, LearnResult still returned
record_learning fails: ? propagates Err; writer drops with empty pending; no warning

failureModes.expectedBehavior: ? propagates Err; no phantom embedding queued

userStories.notes:
The simplest migration. Use it as a template for FEAT-003/004/005.

Current state (clean main): learn() takes (conn, params) with no db_dir. 17 unit tests in the same file call `learn(&conn, params)`. 2 integration tests (tests/e2e_loop.rs, tests/learnings.rs) also call it. All must be updated to pass `None`.

Files to read first:
- src/commands/learn.rs (~650 lines total, function body at 103-160)
- src/main.rs Commands::Learn arm (~line 313-348)
- tests/e2e_loop.rs ~line 186 (search for `learn::learn(`)
- tests/learnings.rs ~line 1028 (search for `learn(` inside the integration tests)
- src/learnings/crud/writer.rs (from FEAT-001, now exists)

Use `git grep 'learn(&conn'` to catch every caller before modifying the signature.

Learning 1195: Read all consumers before refactoring.
Learning 111: Transaction→Connection Deref — not needed here (learn has no tx).
Learning 1271: Engine integration pattern — minimal touch points.

Key validation: after the change, `cargo test` passes clean (no call site missed).

correctness: flush() is called before the Ok(...) return in every code path
performance: One writer per learn() call — minimal overhead
flush on empty pending (db_dir=None in tests) is a cheap no-op
style: Match existing code patterns in learn.rs
Use the top-level re-export `crate::learnings::LearningWriter` if available

userStories.title: Migrate learn command to LearningWriter (adds db_dir param)

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: src/commands/import_learnings/mod.rs imports LearningWriter
Positive: LearningWriter::new(Some(dir)) is constructed BEFORE `let tx = conn.transaction()?;`
Positive: The loop body calls `writer.record(&tx, params)?` — verify grep for `writer.record(&tx`
Positive: After `tx.commit()?`, there is a `let embedded = writer.flush(&conn);` call
Positive: The eprintln log still reports `Embedded N/M imported learning(s).` using the count from flush
Negative: The `created: Vec<(i64, String, String)>` local variable is REMOVED from import_learnings — grep confirms
Negative: The direct call to `try_embed_learnings_batch` is REMOVED from import_learnings
Negative: writer.flush is NOT called before tx.commit — verify line ordering
Positive: All existing tests in src/commands/import_learnings/tests.rs still pass
Positive: `cargo test import_learnings` passes clean
Unit test (new or existing): importing an empty learnings list leaves pending empty and returns 0 imported
Unit test: importing 2 duplicates of an existing learning results in 0 created, 0 embedded, 2 skipped
Known-bad: if flush is called INSIDE the loop (once per learning), every call re-reads ProjectConfig and re-runs the Ollama health check — 50x slower. Verify flush is called ONCE after the loop.
CONTRACT: writer.record accepts `&Transaction` via Deref (learning 111) — verify by passing `&tx` where `&Connection` is expected
Failure mode: if record_learning fails on iteration N, ? propagates; tx implicitly rolls back when dropped; writer drops with pending from iters 0..N (Drop warning fires but records were rolled back)
userStories.description:
Replace the current `created: Vec<(i64,String,String)>` + explicit `try_embed_learnings_batch` dance in src/commands/import_learnings/mod.rs with a LearningWriter. The tx wrapping stays the same — writer.record works fine inside a tx because Ollama calls are deferred to flush().

DO: Construct `LearningWriter::new(Some(dir))` before opening the transaction.
DO: Inside the loop, call `writer.record(&tx, params)?` instead of `record_learning(&tx, params)?`. This is valid because Transaction derefs to Connection (learning 111).
DO: After `tx.commit()?`, call `writer.flush(&conn)` to run the Ollama batch outside the transaction.
DO: Delete the `created: Vec<(i64, String, String)>` collection and the explicit `try_embed_learnings_batch` call — the writer owns both now.
DO: Keep all existing behavior: dedup via HashSet, stats preservation when reset_stats is false, tags counting.
DO NOT: Move Ollama calls inside the transaction — writer.record() does NOT issue Ollama calls, only flush() does, and flush() must run AFTER commit.
DO NOT: Change the function signature of `import_learnings(dir, from_file, reset_stats)` — callers are unchanged.
DO NOT: Remove the `eprintln!("Embedded {}/{} imported...")` log line — move it to use the count from writer.flush().

Edge cases:
- Zero imported (all duplicates skipped): writer has empty pending; flush returns 0; no log output.
- reset_stats=true: stats aren't preserved, but the create path is identical.
- Partial success: if record_learning fails mid-loop, the ? propagates and the entire tx rolls back. Writer drops with pending items (from successful records before the failure) — Drop prints a warning. ACCEPTABLE — those records are rolled back anyway, so the phantom warning count is slightly misleading but not wrong. Document this in a code comment.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Calling writer.flush(&tx) instead of writer.flush(&conn) — after commit, `tx` is consumed/dropped, so this wouldn't even compile. But if flush is called BEFORE tx.commit(), the Ollama call happens while the tx is still open, holding DB locks. Verify flush runs AFTER commit.
- Collecting `created` in addition to writer.pending — would double the memory and tag the refactor as incomplete.
edgeCases: Zero learnings imported (all duplicates): writer.pending empty, flush returns 0
All imports succeed: writer.pending full, flush runs batch Ollama call after commit
Mid-loop record failure: ? propagates, tx rolls back, writer drops with partial pending (Drop warning fires — acceptable since records were rolled back)

failureModes.expectedBehavior: ? propagates Err; tx rolls back; function returns Err; writer drops with pending items from 0..N-1 and prints Drop warning
flush returns 0; import_learnings still returns Ok with the correct imported count; no Err propagated

correctness: writer.flush runs AFTER tx.commit() — Ollama never holds a DB lock
record_learning is still called inside the tx (via writer.record)
Partial failure rolls back via tx drop
performance: Single flush call after the entire batch — batched Ollama HTTP
No duplicate tracking (writer.pending replaces the ad-hoc `created` vec)
style: Delete unused code — do not leave the old `created` vec as dead code
Match existing error-handling style in import_learnings

userStories.title: Migrate import_learnings to LearningWriter

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: DedupParams in src/commands/curate/types.rs has `pub db_dir: Option<std::path::PathBuf>` field, defaulting to None in Default impl — grep confirms
Positive: MergeClusterResult in src/commands/curate/types.rs has `pub merged_title: String` and `pub merged_content: String` fields — grep confirms
Positive: merge_cluster() populates both new fields in the returned MergeClusterResult — verify with a test that calls merge_cluster directly
Positive: LearningWriter has a new `pub fn push_existing(&mut self, learning_id: i64, title: String, content: String)` method
Positive: push_existing is a no-op when db_dir is None (mirrors record behavior — no push to pending)
Positive: curate_dedup() constructs `LearningWriter::new(params.db_dir.as_deref())` and calls writer.push_existing after each successful merge_cluster
Positive: curate_dedup() calls `let _ = writer.flush(conn);` ONCE after the cluster loop, before the final Ok(DedupResult) return
Positive: src/main.rs Commands::Curate::Dedup arm constructs DedupParams with `db_dir: Some(cli.dir.clone())`
Negative: merge_cluster's signature is UNCHANGED — `fn merge_cluster(conn: &Connection, params: MergeClusterParams) -> TaskMgrResult<MergeClusterResult>`
Negative: No direct call to `try_embed_learnings_batch` in curate/mod.rs — only LearningWriter.flush calls it (which lives in writer.rs)
Positive: All 26 existing merge_cluster test call sites in src/commands/curate/tests.rs still compile — any test that destructures MergeClusterResult needs the two new fields added
Positive: All existing tests that construct `DedupParams::default()` still compile and pass (default db_dir is None — no behavior change)
Positive: `cargo test curate` passes clean — especially tests: test_merge_cluster_*, test_curate_dedup_*
Unit test: curate_dedup with DedupParams::default() (db_dir=None, dry_run=false) — merge_cluster runs, writer.pending stays empty, flush returns 0, no eprintln
Unit test: merge_cluster returns MergeClusterResult where merged_title and merged_content match the input MergeClusterParams
Known-bad: if merge_cluster forgets to clone title/content before moving into RecordLearningParams, the result fields can't be populated — the build will fail to compile
Known-bad: if curate_dedup calls writer.flush INSIDE the cluster loop (once per cluster), N Ollama calls happen instead of 1 batched call. Verify flush is called ONCE after the loop
CONTRACT: DedupParams.db_dir is Option<PathBuf>, curate_dedup calls `params.db_dir.as_deref()` to get Option<&Path> for LearningWriter::new
userStories.description:
The most involved migration. merge_cluster does its DB insert inside its own atomic transaction (combined with bandit-stat updates and source retirement), so it cannot take a &mut LearningWriter without breaking 26 curate tests. Instead: (a) add `db_dir: Option<PathBuf>` to DedupParams, (b) add `merged_title`/`merged_content` to MergeClusterResult, (c) have curate_dedup construct a LearningWriter and push_existing after each successful merge.

DO: Add `pub db_dir: Option<std::path::PathBuf>` field to `DedupParams` in src/commands/curate/types.rs. Default to `None` in `impl Default for DedupParams`. This keeps all existing tests that use `DedupParams::default()` working (they get None, writer no-ops).
DO: Add `pub merged_title: String` and `pub merged_content: String` fields to `MergeClusterResult` in src/commands/curate/types.rs.
DO: In merge_cluster() at src/commands/curate/mod.rs:~350, clone `params.merged_title` and `params.merged_content` BEFORE moving them into `RecordLearningParams`. Use the clones to populate the new result fields.
DO: In curate_dedup() at src/commands/curate/mod.rs:~638, construct `LearningWriter::new(params.db_dir.as_deref())` at the top. After each successful `merge_cluster()` call, call `writer.push_existing(result.merged_learning_id, result.merged_title.clone(), result.merged_content.clone())` — clone because `all_clusters.push(DedupCluster { ... })` later also needs the strings. At the end (after the full loop), call `writer.flush(conn)`.
DO: Add a new `push_existing(&mut self, learning_id: i64, title: String, content: String)` method to LearningWriter. Document it clearly: "For callers that manage their own atomic multi-statement transactions (e.g., merge_cluster, which combines insert + bandit-stat updates atomically) and need to queue the embedding without going through `record()`."
DO: Update src/main.rs Commands::Curate::Dedup arm to set `db_dir: Some(cli.dir.clone())` when constructing DedupParams.
DO NOT: Add a &mut LearningWriter parameter to merge_cluster() — 26 tests call merge_cluster directly. Keep its signature (&Connection, MergeClusterParams) -> Result<MergeClusterResult>.
DO NOT: Change MergeClusterParams — only MergeClusterResult grows.
DO NOT: Run Ollama calls inside merge_cluster's transaction.
DO NOT: Break any existing test that calls `curate_dedup(conn, DedupParams::default())` — default db_dir to None.

Edge cases:
- params.db_dir = None (tests use DedupParams::default()): writer is constructed with None; push_existing is a no-op; flush returns 0. 26 merge_cluster tests continue to pass because merge_cluster's signature is unchanged.
- dry_run=true: curate_dedup never calls merge_cluster; writer.pending stays empty; flush returns 0.
- Empty cluster list: same as dry_run — flush returns 0.
- Partial merge failure: curate_dedup's loop has `eprintln!("Warning: merge_cluster failed: {}", e); continue;` — failed clusters are skipped. Writer only receives successful merges. ACCEPTABLE.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Pushing to writer BEFORE calling merge_cluster (e.g., from the cluster plan) — the merged_learning_id wouldn't exist yet. Push AFTER successful merge_cluster() returns.
- Forgetting to update MergeClusterResult pattern destructures in tests — search curate/tests.rs for `MergeClusterResult { `.
- Calling writer.record() in curate_dedup — no, merge_cluster already did the record via record_learning inside its own tx. Use push_existing.
edgeCases: db_dir=None (test default): writer no-ops; 0 embedded; merge_cluster tests unchanged
dry_run=true: merge_cluster never called; flush returns 0
Partial merge failure: writer only holds successful clusters

failureModes.expectedBehavior: curate_dedup's existing warning + continue logic kicks in; writer does NOT receive a push_existing for N; flush only processes successful clusters
flush returns 0; curate_dedup still returns the DedupResult with correct clusters_found / learnings_merged / learnings_created counts

correctness: merge_cluster's tx is unchanged — insert + bandit + retire all atomic
writer.flush runs OUTSIDE merge_cluster's tx (after the whole cluster loop)
26 merge_cluster tests still compile and pass (signature unchanged)
MergeClusterResult destructures in tests are updated with the two new fields
performance: Single writer.flush call per curate_dedup run — one batched Ollama call
style: Delete the ad-hoc embedding block — do not leave it commented out
Group push_existing next to record in the impl block
Doc comment explaining why push_existing exists (merge_cluster's atomic multi-statement tx)

userStories.title: Migrate curate_dedup/merge_cluster to LearningWriter (adds DedupParams.db_dir + MergeClusterResult fields)

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: extract_learnings_from_output signature is `fn extract_learnings_from_output(conn: &Connection, output: &str, task_id: Option<&str>, run_id: Option<&str>, db_dir: Option<&Path>) -> TaskMgrResult<ExtractionResult>` — db_dir added as 5th param
Positive: Inside the function, `LearningWriter::new(db_dir)` is constructed AFTER parse_extraction_response succeeds and BEFORE the record loop
Positive: The loop body calls `writer.record(conn, params)` instead of `record_learning(conn, params)` — grep confirms
Positive: After the loop, `writer.flush(conn)` is called before returning Ok(ExtractionResult)
Positive: src/loop_engine/engine.rs:~639 passes `Some(params.db_dir)` — grep confirms
Positive: src/main.rs ExtractLearnings command arm passes `Some(&cli.dir)` — grep confirms
Positive: All existing tests in src/learnings/ingestion/ pass with updated signatures (test callers pass `None`)
Positive: `cargo test learnings::ingestion` passes clean
Positive: `cargo test loop_engine` passes clean
Negative: No direct `record_learning(conn, params)` call remains in extract_learnings_from_output — grep confirms
Negative: writer.flush is NOT called inside the record loop — only ONCE after it
Unit test: extract_learnings_from_output with db_dir=None still records learnings and returns ExtractionResult with the correct learning_ids — same as pre-refactor behavior

Known-bad: if the loop engine passes None instead of Some(params.db_dir), the whole refactor is neutralized for the main production path. Verify with a grep: `extract_learnings_from_output` in engine.rs must pass Some(_) for db_dir.
CONTRACT: LoopExecutionParams in src/loop_engine/engine.rs has `pub db_dir: &'a Path` (line ~106) — verify by reading the struct; the call site passes `Some(params.db_dir)`

Failure mode: individual record_learning failure inside the loop is already logged via eprintln!+continue; writer.record wraps that — the ? would propagate and break the loop. Instead, match on writer.record's result and preserve the continue behavior.
userStories.description:
Close the silent gap: extract_learnings_from_output in src/learnings/ingestion/mod.rs currently calls record_learning in a loop with NO embedding step. This is the path used by the loop engine after every agent iteration — embeddings for loop-extracted learnings only get created when someone manually runs `curate embed`. After this refactor, loop-extracted learnings get embeddings automatically.

DO: Add a `db_dir: Option<&Path>` parameter to extract_learnings_from_output() in src/learnings/ingestion/mod.rs:64. Use Option so existing test callers can pass None.
DO: Inside extract_learnings_from_output, construct `LearningWriter::new(db_dir)` before the record loop. Replace `record_learning(conn, params)` with `writer.record(conn, params)`. After the loop, call `writer.flush(conn)`.
DO: Update the caller in src/loop_engine/engine.rs:~639 to pass `Some(params.db_dir)` — the LoopExecutionParams struct already has `db_dir: &Path`.
DO: Update the caller in src/main.rs (the ExtractLearnings command arm, around line 939) to pass `Some(&cli.dir)`.
DO: Any tests in src/learnings/ingestion/ that call extract_learnings_from_output need the new parameter added — pass None.
DO NOT: Change the error handling (`eprintln!("Warning: failed to record extracted learning: {}"`)) — keep it. Individual record failures log and continue.
DO NOT: Move the Claude LLM spawn_claude call or the enrichment logic — only the record loop + flush changes.
DO NOT: Make db_dir required (non-Option) — unit tests in src/learnings/ingestion/tests.rs may not have a db_dir.

Edge cases:
- db_dir=None (unit tests): writer no-ops; behavior matches pre-refactor (records only, no embeddings).
- LLM returns 0 learnings: existing early return; writer is constructed but never used; flush returns 0.
- Individual record fails but others succeed: writer.record returns Err for that one; existing eprintln!+continue logic logs it; writer continues for subsequent records. Embedding is queued only for successful records.
- Ollama down at flush time: graceful no-op; loop engine sees successful extraction; embeddings backfill later via curate embed.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Placing writer.flush INSIDE the Claude spawn or parse block — it must run after the record loop completes.
- Passing `None` from the loop engine accidentally — this would make loop-extracted learnings still skip embeddings. Verify engine.rs passes `Some(params.db_dir)`.
- Test callers not updated — verify `cargo test learnings::ingestion` passes.
edgeCases: db_dir=None: record loop runs as before; flush no-op; embeddings skipped
db_dir=Some but Ollama down: flush logs warning, returns 0; learning_ids still populated
LLM returns empty params_list: existing early return; writer never touched
Individual record_learning fails: matched, logged, loop continues; only successful records queued

failureModes.cause: One record_learning call fails in the middle of the loop
failureModes.expectedBehavior: eprintln warning, continue loop; writer only holds successful records; flush runs over successful subset

failureModes.cause: Loop engine call site passes None
failureModes.expectedBehavior: BUG — verify the engine.rs call site explicitly passes `Some(params.db_dir)`; this is a regression risk

correctness: Per-learning error handling is preserved (log + continue, not ?)
db_dir is passed from loop engine via params.db_dir
writer.flush runs AFTER the record loop, before the function returns
Test callers pass None and still work
performance: Single batched Ollama call at the end of extraction
No per-learning Ollama overhead inside the loop
style: Preserve existing eprintln formatting for backward compat
Parameter order: db_dir as the LAST parameter (5th), so it's additive

userStories.title: Migrate extract_learnings_from_output to LearningWriter (loop engine path)

acceptanceCriteria: No duplicate embedding logic across learn.rs, import_learnings, curate_dedup, ingestion — all route through LearningWriter
Grep `record_learning(` in src/commands/ and src/learnings/ingestion/ returns only: the writer's internal call, and any test-helper/setup code. Production paths go through writer.record.
Grep `try_embed_learnings_batch` returns only: its definition in embeddings/mod.rs and the call inside LearningWriter.flush. No production path calls it directly.
LearningWriter has rustdoc explaining: (a) the tx rule, (b) that it's the production chokepoint, (c) push_existing's purpose for atomic multi-statement tx callers
Functions under 40 lines (flag any >40 lines introduced by this refactor)
Clear separation: LearningWriter in src/learnings/crud/writer.rs; callers in src/commands/ and src/learnings/ingestion/
cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean
cargo fmt --check clean
userStories.description:
Review all modified files for DRY violations, dead code, doc drift, and chokepoint leaks. The LearningWriter is the common interface — verify no production path bypasses it.

DO: Grep src/commands/ and src/learnings/ingestion/ for direct calls to `record_learning(` — only LearningWriter's internal implementation should call it (and tests + src/learnings/crud/ itself).
DO: Grep for `try_embed_learning` and `try_embed_learnings_batch` — only LearningWriter.flush should call the batch variant; the singular variant may still be useful as a lower-level primitive but should not be called from any production path.
DO: Check that the LearningWriter doc comments clearly explain the tx rule (Ollama never inside tx) and point future devs at the writer as the production chokepoint.
DO: Check function lengths — if any modified function is >40 lines due to the refactor, consider extraction.
DO: Run the simplify skill (if available) for a fresh-eyes pass.
DO NOT: Refactor code not touched by this PR — scope creep.
DO NOT: Mark passes=true without actually running cargo clippy -- -D warnings and cargo fmt --check.
userStories.notes:
If issues found: add REFACTOR-FIX-xxx tasks to JSON (priority 50-97), commit JSON changes.

If clean: mark passes:true with note 'No refactoring needed — LearningWriter is the sole production chokepoint; verified by grep.'

Learning 488/487: Skip extraction when SRP benefit is marginal — don't split LearningWriter into sub-modules if it fits in one ~150 line file. Only extract if the refactor reveals a genuinely independent concern.
Learning 880: Inline orchestrator blocks are the real refactoring targets — check that curate_dedup's orchestration (batch loop + merge loop) wasn't left bloated.
Learning 454: Module extraction workflow: write-edit-register-repoint.
userStories.title: Review for refactoring opportunities across the 5 migrations

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: All new code reachable from production entry points (learn/import-learnings/curate dedup/loop iteration)
Positive: cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check all clean
Positive: ~2854 existing tests + new LearningWriter tests all pass — verify via `grep '^test result' /tmp/test-results.txt`
Positive: Manual smoke test: `task-mgr learn ...` on a DB with Ollama running results in an embedding stored (verify via `curate embed --status` delta)
Positive: Manual smoke test: `task-mgr import-learnings --from-json <file>` produces embeddings for newly imported learnings
Positive: Manual smoke test: `task-mgr curate dedup` (non-dry-run) produces embeddings for merged learnings
Positive: CLAUDE.md updated with a 'Learning creation chokepoint' section pointing at src/learnings/crud/writer.rs and documenting the tx rule
Negative: No unwrap() in production code paths (tests OK)
Negative: No dead code warnings (unused `created` vec, unused imports of try_embed_learning, etc.)
Negative: No production path bypasses LearningWriter — grep confirms
Documentation: doc drift in src/learnings/embeddings/mod.rs addressed (if try_embed_learning is no longer called from production, note it as 'primitive used only by LearningWriter.flush')
Task update: Remaining tasks reviewed; progress.txt has a summary of what changed and why
If issues found: FIX-xxx tasks added to JSON with priority 50-97
userStories.description:
Final review of all changes. Verify: (1) every production learning-creation path goes through LearningWriter; (2) no Ollama HTTP call happens inside a rusqlite Transaction; (3) graceful degradation works when Ollama is down; (4) documentation reflects the new pattern.

DO: Read every touched file and verify each acceptance criterion from the FEAT tasks.
DO: Run the full test suite — cargo test 2>&1 | tee /tmp/test-results.txt | grep '^test result' — verify ~2854 tests still pass.
DO: Manual smoke tests (if Ollama is running locally): (a) `task-mgr learn --outcome pattern --title test --content test`, (b) `task-mgr curate embed --status` — embed count should increment; (c) run a short loop iteration that triggers extract_learnings_from_output and check the extracted learning got an embedding.
DO: Update CLAUDE.md with a 'Learning creation chokepoint' note: point future devs at LearningWriter in src/learnings/crud/writer.rs as the required entry point for any new creation path.
DO: Check for documentation drift in src/learnings/embeddings/mod.rs — if the 'best-effort inline embedding' section's doc comments reference try_embed_learning directly and those functions are no longer called from production, update them.
DO: Review remaining tasks — if implementation changed APIs, update remaining task descriptions/criteria to match reality (e.g., if push_existing was renamed).
DO NOT: Skip the integration wiring check — unconnected code is the #1 failure mode. Verify LearningWriter is actually reached from main.rs and engine.rs via grep traces.
DO NOT: Skip the manual smoke test of curate embed --status before/after a learn command.
userStories.notes:
If issues found: add FIX-xxx tasks to JSON, commit JSON changes. Loop picks them up automatically.

If no issues: mark passes:true with note 'Clean review — LearningWriter is the sole production chokepoint; all paths migrated; manual smoke tests verified embedding creation for learn/import/dedup/loop-extraction.'

Manual smoke test commands (require Ollama running):
```bash
task-mgr curate embed --status  # note count
task-mgr learn --outcome pattern --title 'writer smoke' --content 'verify writer path'
task-mgr curate embed --status  # should be +1
```

Learning 824/1244: cargo test may surface warnings that cargo clippy didn't — run both.
Learning 693: `.expect("invariant")` is acceptable in production when the invariant is genuinely upheld; `.unwrap()` is not.
Learning 1426: CLAUDE.md has an Embedding/Ollama config section — extend it rather than duplicating.
userStories.title: Code review + final verification + documentation update
